fresh_facts.claim: Approximately 2,500 U.S. Marines are being deployed to the Middle East along with a Navy amphibious warship as of March 13, 2026.
The Pentagon has struck more than 15,000 targets in Iran over nearly two weeks of relentless bombing as of March 13, 2026.
U.S. military death toll has risen to 13 personnel, with six service members confirmed deceased when their aircraft was lost in western Iraq as of March 13, 2026.
Iran targeted a U.S. Air base in southern Turkey with a ballistic missile in the early hours of Friday, March 13, 2026, though the missile was intercepted by NATO defenses.
Iran's government reports that more than 2,000 people have been killed since the start of the war, including more than 170 in a missile strike on an elementary school as of March 13, 2026.
Large crowds gathered in Tehran for Al-Quds Day on March 13, 2026, where demonstrators chanted anti-American and anti-Israeli slogans while setting fire to flags.
Iran continues attacking Persian Gulf shipping and energy infrastructure, with Iranian threats choking global oil shipments as of March 13, 2026.
Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth stated the U.S. does not target civilians, though the Pentagon is investigating allegations regarding the school strike as of March 13, 2026.

Mojtaba Khamenei issued his first statement as Supreme Leader on March 12, 2026, following his selection by the Assembly of Experts on March 8 after his father Ali Khamenei's assassination on February 28.

Mojtaba Khamenei reaffirmed Iran's war commitments in his first statement, including disrupting the Strait of Hormuz, attacking regional U.S.-host countries, and supporting the Axis of Resistance on March 12, 2026.
An elite NOPO counterterrorism unit was deployed to protect Mojtaba Khamenei following his father's assassination on February 28, 2026.
US-Israeli strikes targeted Iranian IRGC and military sites in ten provinces since March 12 morning ET, including IRGC Ground Forces in Khuzestan as of March 13, 2026.
Anti-regime reports cited fractures between Artesh and IRGC due to supply shortages, with IRGC refusing aid to wounded Artesh troops as of March 12, 2026.
Failed reserve mobilizations were reported between Artesh and IRGC as of March 12, 2026.
Iran escalated shipping and infrastructure strikes, including attacks on two foreign oil tankers as of March 12, 2026.
Ali Khamenei was assassinated on February 28, 2026, leading to a leadership succession.
The Assembly of Experts selected Mojtaba Khamenei as the new Supreme Leader on March 8, 2026.

regime_fall.current_status: Operation Epic Fury has intensified into its fourth day with over 15,000 Pentagon strikes targeting Iranian IRGC and military sites across ten provinces, 2,500 additional Marines deployed, and Iran launching ballistic missiles at NATO bases and escalating attacks on Persian Gulf shipping. Mojtaba Khamenei's succession is consolidated with his reaffirmation of war commitments, while internal military fractures between Artesh and IRGC over supply shortages suggest structural strain despite outward regime continuity.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Pentagon announces it has struck over 15,000 targets in Iran over nearly two weeks and deploys approximately 2,500 Marines to the Middle East as of March 13, 2026.
